videos.title: The fastest way to make an AI agent dangerous #AIagents #AI #agents #automation #futureofwork
videos.description:
The fastest way to make an AI agent dangerous is simple. Let everyone use it and nobody own it.

That is where it breaks. Not some rogue AI. An agent that can read your files, draft messages, change code, and update records, with nobody whose actual job is to check it. The output looks clean, so people stop asking where it came from.

Ownership is the missing skill. 

I put the one-page owner card and the two prompts that fix this on Substack.

Full breakdown: https://natesnewsletter.substack.co

videos.title: I Built My Own AI Memory by Talking to Claude. It Did 80% Itself.
videos.description:
The Full Open Stack Guide:
https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/build-your-own-ai-memory?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

AI agents can now build most of your own AI memory stack for you, just by talking to Claude or Codex.
This is how to build a personal agent that starts from your context, follows your intent, and waits for your yes before it acts.

videos.title: Midjourney breaks into ... healthcare? #AI #Medtech #MidjourneyMedical #Medicalbreakthrough
videos.description:
Midjourney medical is a real thing now. The AI image company built a full-body ultrasound.

It is cheaper than an MRI and faster. They want to run a billion scans a year, and apparently it is built to feel like a trip to the spa. The whole thing came out of a 40-person team that funds itself off the image business.

Here is the part that gets me. The most interesting AI move of the week did not come from OpenAI or Anthropic. It came from the company you thought only made pictures.

That is the s

videos.title: Nobody’s ready for Claude Fable 5 #Anthropic #AI #Fable5 #Claude
videos.description:
Claude Fable 5 was public for a few days, and the strange part was not just how good it was. It was realizing the new constraint might be us.

The model didn't run out of ability. I ran out of big things to ask for.

So the skill that matters now isn't prompting — it's learning to ask bigger. That's what the full breakdown gets into: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/claude-fable-5-how-to-use?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true


My Links 🔗 Newslett

videos.title: Fable Is Back: Here's What You Should Try First
videos.description:
OpenAI and other labs are racing to slash inference costs, prompting debate about token-efficiency techniques and quality trade-offs. Anthropic regained global access to Fable 5 after export controls were lifted, triggering discussions about jailbreaking, tightened guardrails, and regulatory opacity. New model launches and platform moves include Sonnet 5's agentic-but-costly approach, Base44's fine-tuned Base1, and AWS's billion-dollar FTE push for enterprise AI.

The AI Daily Brief helps you un

videos.title: How Big is the AI Economy
videos.description:
Alleged credit-based, identity-verified access for Anthropic Fable sparks privacy and policy debate. Senate draft targets agent neutrality and a duty-of-loyalty while state and corporate contracts shift pricing and access. Exponential View estimates a $175 billion annual AI run rate and documents a compute supercycle with surging token volumes and falling unit token prices.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
